title: Customer Learning Program Playbook
description: Design a customer learning program (short tracks, not a real school) tied to a clear warranty experience: what is covered, how to get help, how to self-serve basics. Enable sales to distribute. Measure deflection and satisfaction on real issues.

mindset.principle: Produce-aisle universities work because they teach without shame. Same for hardware and support: educate so the phone is for hard problems, and warranty feels like a promise not a trap.

mistakes.label: Warranty lives only in legal PDF
Build a huge LMS nobody finishes
Great material never reaches sales or customers

program_parts.label: Short learning tracks: first boot, care, when to call, warranty path
Human warranty summary: covered, not covered, how to open a case
One pack sales can send or leave with customer
Basics volume down; CSAT on complex up

decision_branches.situation: Need customer education program + clearer warranty story

agent_signal.use_when: Need customer education university-style program and clearer warranty experience. Sales should hand materials. Basics still flooding support. No third-party brand slogans.
decision_criteria: Keep tracks short; avoid LMS bloat.
Warranty human card aligned with real policy.
Sales distribution is part of launch, not afterthought.
agent_signal.operator_context: Field pattern: warranty revamp plus customer U concept (generic naming). Full circle with preventable tickets and knowledge repo.
agent_signal.not_for: Legal warranty drafting without counsel. Not another company's brand program.
trigger_phrases: Need customer education university-style program and clearer warranty experience
Need playbook: Customer Learning Program Playbook
